channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump unleashes a supercharged MAGA agenda and Republicans come aboard
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump is testing how far Republicans are willing to go in supporting his supercharged “Make America Great Again” agenda, tearing down government agencies and taking actions fundamentally at odds with once-traditional party principles.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Video: 2 men facing charges after Albuquerque courtroom brawl
item.description: Video of the encounter, some of which can be seen in the video player above, shows the defendant standing in front of the judge when a man appears to jump over the partition and run forward.
item.dc:creator: Chandler Farnsworth, NewsNation

item.title: Honolulu records 6th death from massive explosion of illegal fireworks over New Year's
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A sixth person died Wednesday from injuries sustained when crates of illegal fireworks ignited during a New Year’s Eve party in a Honolulu neighborhood, setting off a chain of explosions that left more than a dozen people with severe burns.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police seek motive after a suspect was arrested for a fatal shooting at an Ohio cosmetics warehouse
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A man accused of shooting to death a coworker and wounding five others at an Ohio warehouse has spoken with police as they probe for a motive, authorities said Wednesday, hours after he was arrested in an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Chinese migrant on paddleboard with suitcase in tow apprehended near Bahamas days after separate Florida bust
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard found and apprehended a Chinese migrant floating on a pair of paddleboards off the Bahamas, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Turner to lead HUD
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed Scott Turner to lead the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), cementing another one of President Trump’s Cabinet choices. Senators voted 55-44 on Turner’s nomination. Sens. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) and Peter Welch (D-Vt.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to sign executive order barring transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order Wednesday banning transgender athletes from competing in girls’ and women’s sports, the latest in a string of executive actions from the new administration to take aim at LGBTQ rights.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KTLA 5

item.title: Bondi sworn in as attorney general in Oval Office
item.description: Pam Bondi was sworn in as attorney general on Wednesday in the Oval Office with President Trump looking on and telling reporters she would be “as impartial as a person can be." “I know I'm supposed to say, ‘She's going to be totally impartial with respect to Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge cements block on Trump’s order restricting birthright citizenship
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. — A federal judge Wednesday furthered a block of President Trump’s executive order preventing the children of migrants without legal status from receiving birthright citizenship. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Mahomes reveals thoughts on Trump attending the Super Bowl
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes was asked what it will feel like to play in front of a sitting president.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Petrulis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service flip-flops on Hong Kong-China packages, lifting a ban imposed a day earlier
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — The U.S. Postal Service is reversing course a day after placing a ban on all inbound packages from China and Hong Kong. The post office had announced Tuesday that it would no longer accept parcels from the China and Hong Kong after the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Senate Dems ask Hegseth to probe potential price gouging on military families' housing
item.description: “The Department of Defense has a responsibility to protect military families from predatory private housing companies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Shutdown fears grow as Trump battles multiply
item.description: The odds of a government shutdown are surging as President Trump battles Democrats over efforts to freeze funding and Republicans brawl internally over the size and scope of potential cuts.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Celebrities rail against Trump's policies
item.description: After years of launching blistering attacks on President Donald Trump, much of Hollywood appears to be casting him in a new "Harry Potter"-inspired role: He Who Must Not Be Named.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Protests against Trump, Project 2025 planned in California
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk shocks lawmakers, setting himself on collision course
item.description: Billionaire businessman Elon Musk is on a collision course with lawmakers on Capitol Hill who are starting to challenge his authority.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Chilean migrant gangs terrorize Americans with home invasions: what to know
item.description: A law enforcement veteran describes how people can defend themselves after Chilean migrant burglary rings continue to target American homeowners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baltimore mayor fights back against attacks on DEI initiatives with ‘Definitely Earned It’ campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's attack on diversity, equity and inclusion programs have led military and government agencies — along with some private companies — to roll back observances of diversity and celebrations of Black History Month, which goes through February.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis goes to battle with Florida Republicans in trying to get closer to Trump
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — With an open presidential primary coming in 2028, Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is launching an offensive to win back supporters of the Republican leader whom he dared to challenge in the last election.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USAID direct hires put on leave worldwide, except those deemed essential
item.description: The Trump administration is placing U.S. Agency for International Development direct-hire staffers around the world on leave, except those deemed essential.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Trump says he wants US to take charge of Gaza Strip at press conference with Netanyahu
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he wants the U.S. to take ownership of the Gaza Strip and redevelop it after Palestinians are resettled elsewhere. He made the remarks in a joint press conference with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Video captures courtroom brawl as two men attack murder suspect during hearing
item.description: Video captured the chaotic moment two men started a brawl with murder suspect Alexander Ortiz during his hearing inside a New Mexico courtroom.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials confirm all 67 victims in DC midair crash recovered from Potomac River
item.description: Officials confirmed Tuesday that all 67 victims in the DC midair crash have been recovered from the Potomac River, with 66 bodies positively identified.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why it's good for NASA that an asteroid carved out 2 grand canyons on the moon
item.description: New research shows that when an asteroid slammed into the moon billions of years ago, it carved out a pair of grand canyons on the lunar far side.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trayon White expelled from the DC Council for corruption but he's free to run for his old seat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The D.C. Council voted unanimously Tuesday to expel Councilmember Trayon White, who faces a federal trial on charges of taking tens of thousands of dollars in bribes to influence city contract decisions. White, 40, was arrested by the FBI last August.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders acquittal of ex-prosecutor on 1 of 2 counts in misconduct trial over Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — A judge on Monday ordered the acquittal of a former Georgia prosecutor on one of the two misconduct charges against her, ruling that prosecutors at her trial failed to present any evidence the she obstructed a police investigation of the 2020 killing of Ahmaud .Arbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MLB ump fired amid gambling probe
item.description: Major League Baseball has fired an umpire as part of an investigation into sports gambling. Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball, according to ESPN reporter Jesse Rogers and later confirmed by the league.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: New York shields abortion pill prescribers after a doctor was indicted in Louisiana
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul on Monday signed a bill to shield the identities of doctors who prescribe abortion medications, days after a physician in the state was charged with prescribing abortion pills to a pregnant minor in Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Who gets more disaster aid? Republican states. Here's the breakdown
item.description: Experts say every state gets millions in federal help — and a little more goes to Republican states and congressional districts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Mexican president agree to delay tariffs for one month
item.description: President Trump and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said they have agreed to delay tariffs by at least one month, following a call between the two leaders Monday. The 25 percent tariffs on Mexico were to be implemented Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Doncic releases emotional farewell message to Dallas after trade sends him to Lakers
item.description: Luka Doncic released an emotional farewell to Dallas on Sunday, the same day a trade sending him from the Mavericks to the Los Angeles Lakers in a swap for fellow All-NBA player Anthony Davis was finalized.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5
